steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
Lines $m$ and $n$ are parallel, and line $t$ is a transversal cutting through both.

At the intersection of $t$ and line $m$, an angle of $134°$ is marked on the left side below the line.

At the intersection of $t$ and line $n$, angle $w°$ is marked on the left side above the line.

Since both marked angles are on the same side of the transversal and both lie in the interior region between the parallel lines, they are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the co-interior angle property
steps.content:
When a transversal crosses two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary — they sum to $180°$. Setting up the equation:
$$134° + w° = 180°$$
Solving for $w$:
$$w° = 180° - 134° = 46°$$

steps.title: Find the exterior angle
steps.content:
The angle we calculated ($46°$) is the interior angle at line $n$.

However, angle $w$ as marked in the diagram may represent the exterior angle on the other side of line $n$ from the $46°$ interior angle.

This exterior angle is supplementary to the $46°$ interior angle: $$w° = 180° - 46° = 134°$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
